verdict: VERIFIED-CLOSED-RECOMMENDED (for the exact reported scope of NF-final-pass-001) — WITH ONE NEW FINDING in the same class (NF-final-pass-002, caseless-script residual, LOW)
closure_caveat: The 5 SPECIFICALLY REPORTED gluings + reverse-order variants + all named controls are clean on prod @c158729, reproduced across TWO independent jobs and TWO independent sessions plus a real-browser panel render — so NF-final-pass-001's enumerated reproduction genuinely no longer reproduces and is closeable. HOWEVER, my own novel adversarial proves the fix's structural-termination claim is FALSE: a CASELESS-script proper noun (CJK / Devanagari / Arabic / Korean-Katakana / Hebrew / Thai ...) glued to an ASCII label word STILL leaks as a keyword chip via the IDENTICAL len(segments)<2 early-return that NF-final-pass-001 itself named. NF-final-pass-001's own acceptance criterion is 'No concatenation artifact surfaces ... REGARDLESS of which proper noun ... is glued to the label' — a strict reading of that criterion is still violated. Orchestrator to adjudicate: (a) close NF-final-pass-001 scoped to the reported cased-script cases + track NF-final-pass-002 as the caseless residual (mirrors the accented->non-Latin residual chain), or (b) hold NF-final-pass-001 open until the caseless case is also fixed. GAPS.json NOT edited by this agent.

novel_adversarial.design: My own design, same class, targeting the fix's explicit structural claim. Rather than another CASED non-Latin script (already covered by _camel_humps' ch.isupper()), I glued CASELESS-script proper nouns to ASCII label words — the exact case the fix commit asserts 'genuinely ends here structurally' because 'a caseless character can neither start nor continue a hump — it is simply skipped'.
novel_adversarial.outcome: LEAKED — new finding. Job A4 chips (prod @c158729, both passes + real browser panel): [Terraform, Docker, القاهرةSalary, बईLocation, ソウルSalary, 東京Salary, Engineer, Systems, competitive, quarterly]. Three caseless gluings surfaced intact (東京Salary, القاهرةSalary, ソウルSalary) and the Devanagari one surfaced as a garbage fragment 'बईLocation'. The structural fix does NOT drop these.

new_findings.class: residual of NF-final-pass-001 / NF-final-closure-001 / NF-final-PII-001 / MV-cover-letter-studio-006 — the CASELESS-SCRIPT proper-noun case (CJK / Devanagari / Arabic / Korean-Katakana / Hebrew / Thai / ...) that the unicode-CASE-based _camel_humps segmenter does not cover
new_findings.epistemic: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE] leak observed on prod @c158729 (insights API x2 sessions + real-browser KeywordCoveragePanel); [INFERRED] root cause from deployed-source reading + local predictor
new_findings.summary: JD Keyword Coverage still leaks a CamelCase concatenation artifact when the glued proper noun is written in a CASELESS script and glued to an ASCII label word — e.g. a scraped JD gluing a city to a label surfaces '東京Salary', 'القاهرةSalary', 'ソウルSalary' as top keyword chips on the paid intelligence rail (Devanagari 'मुंबईLocation' surfaces as the garbage fragment 'बईLocation'). NF-final-pass-001's _camel_humps rewrite made hump-recognition Unicode-CASE-correct (so Cyrillic/Greek/Turkish-İ proper nouns now form their own hump and get flagged), but a CASELESS letter has no upper/lower distinction, so ch.isupper() and ch.islower() are both False and the character is SKIPPED like punctuation — contributing NO hump. The glued ASCII label ('Salary'/'Location') is then the SOLE segment, so _is_camel_concatenation_artifact hits `len(segments) < 2` and early-returns False (not an artifact) BEFORE the any-segment-is-a-label check runs. Same early-return NF-final-pass-001 and NF-final-closure-001 both named — now reached via a THIRD tokenizer path.
reproduction: 1. Log in as a Pro user with a résumé.

2. Create/seed a Job whose scraped-style description glues a caseless-script city to an ASCII label with no space — e.g. '東京Salary competitive. मुंबईLocation hybrid. القاهرةSalary quarterly. ソウルSalary flexible.' plus a couple legit tech terms so the panel populates.
3. Attach/generate a cover letter for that job.
4. GET /cover-letters/{id}/insights → keywords.items CONTAINS '東京Salary','القاهرةSalary','ソウルSalary' (and 'बईLocation') as top-ranked chips.
5. Open the letter in Cover Letter Studio → the JD Keyword Coverage panel renders the same artifact chips (screenshots/panel-A4-novel-caseless.png).
new_findings.expected: No concatenation artifact surfaces as a keyword regardless of which proper noun (including CASELESS scripts) is glued to the label; legit mixed-case tech preserved. (This is NF-final-pass-001's own acceptance criterion.)
new_findings.observed: 東京Salary + القاهرةSalary + ソウルSalary leaked as top chips (job A4, prod @c158729, pass1 API + pass2 fresh-session re-GET + real-browser panel — all identical). Devanagari मुंबईLocation leaked as the fragment 'बईLocation'. The reported CASED gluings (5 forward + 5 reverse) and precomposed-Latin controls (MünchenLocation) were correctly DROPPED in the same run — isolating the residual to CASELESS scripts specifically. Reproduced deterministically twice; INFERRED-confirmed for 北京Location/東京Location/ソウルSalary by the local deployed-code predictor (api/local-predictor-INFERRED.txt).
new_findings.root_cause_inferred: apps/api/app/routers/cover_letters.py::_camel_humps walks characters using ch.isupper()/ch.islower()/ch.isdigit(). For a caseless-script character all three are False, so `if not ch.isupper(): i += 1; continue` skips it (identical to how punctuation is skipped). A token like '東京Salary' therefore yields exactly ONE hump ['Salary']; _is_camel_concatenation_artifact returns False at `if len(segments) < 2: return False` before `any(seg.lower() in _ARTIFACT_LABEL_WORDS ...)` runs, so _is_semantic_keyword keeps the token and _skill_score awards +45 internal-uppercase (the ASCII 'S') — ranking the artifact above generic prose. The fix commit's rationale ('Caseless scripts (CJK, etc.) ... a caseless character can neither start nor continue a hump — it is simply skipped ... meaning the class genuinely ends here structurally') is precisely the defect: skipping the caseless proper-noun side leaves the ASCII label as the sole segment, so the gluing survives.
new_findings.fix_hint: Any of: (a) in _is_camel_concatenation_artifact, when the token's sole CamelCase segment is a label word BUT the token contains additional letters (caseless script) outside that segment, flag it — i.e. drop/relax the len(segments)<2 gate for the label-word branch when leading/trailing non-hump letters exist; or (b) have _camel_humps emit a run of consecutive caseless letters as its own segment so a caseless-noun+label token yields 2 segments and hits the existing label rule; or (c) detect a token that mixes a caseless-script run with a trailing/leading ASCII label word and treat that mix as a gluing artifact directly. Also fix the Devanagari tokenizer split that leaves the 'बईLocation' fragment (combining vowel signs/anusvara dropped by _WORD_RE).
new_findings.materiality: Narrow per instance (LOW): requires a caseless-script place/proper name directly glued to a label word in a scraped JD; degrades one keyword chip on the paid JD-Keyword-Coverage rail — no PII, no security, no fabricated user content (the chip is a genuine JD token, just an ugly glued one). BUT the trigger surface is BROADER than NF-final-pass-001's cased-script residual: caseless scripts cover CJK (Chinese/Japanese), Korean Hangul, Arabic, Hebrew, Devanagari (Hindi/Marathi), Thai and more — a large share of international job boards. It directly refutes the fix's stated structural-termination guarantee and violates NF-final-pass-001's own 'regardless of which proper noun' acceptance criterion. Filed to keep the fixers honest against that criterion; the brief explicitly asked for a novel unicode adversarial and this is the result.
